title: Spin-Hall-Like Magnon Transport in a Synthetic Antiferromagnetic Skyrmion Lattice
authors: Xingen Zheng, Xuejuan Liu, Zhenyu Wang, Jiyong Kang, Zhixiong Li, Hao Wu
abstract: We investigate spin-Hall-like magnon edge transport in a synthetic antiferromagnetic skyrmion lattice composed of two antiferromagnetically coupled skyrmion lattice layers with opposite magnetic textures. Based on a relaxed bilayer texture from micromagnetic simulations, we construct the bosonic Bogoliubov-de Gennes Hamiltonian within linear spin-wave theory and calculate the bulk and strip magnon spectrum. We find counterpropagating in-gap edge modes with opposite layer polarization, whose layer-resolved propagation is further confirmed by dynamical micromagnetic simulations. A symmetry analysis shows that the fully coupled system lacks the pseudo-time-reversal symmetry required for a genuine bosonic Z2 topological phase. Thus, the observed edge modes are not Z2-protected helical magnon edge states, but layer-polarized, spin-Hall-like modes originating from the opposite Hall tendencies of the two skyrmion lattice layers. These results establish synthetic antiferromagnetic skyrmion lattices as a platform for spin-Hall-like magnon transport beyond a strict bosonic Z2 classification.
summary: This paper explores magnon transport in a bilayer system of coupled skyrmion lattices with opposite textures. It demonstrates that while the system lacks the symmetry for a $Z_2$ topological phase, it successfully hosts layer-polarized, spin-Hall-like edge modes. This provides a new platform for studying bosonic Hall-type transport phenomena.
main_problem: The study investigates whether a synthetic antiferromagnetic skyrmion lattice (SAF-SkL) can realize a bosonic analogue of the spin-Hall effect and determines if its edge modes are $Z_2$-protected helical states or layer-polarized spin-Hall-like modes.
main_result: The researchers find that the SAF-SkL supports counterpropagating, layer-polarized edge modes that are not $Z_2$-protected due to the lack of pseudo-time-reversal symmetry, but instead function as 'spin-Hall-like' modes arising from the opposite Hall tendencies of the two layers.
method: The study employs linear spin-wave theory using the Holstein-Primakoff transformation to derive a bosonic Bogoliubov-de Gennes Hamiltonian, supplemented by micromagnetic simulations (MuMax3) and symmetry analysis.
model_or_system: A synthetic antiferromagnetic skyrmion lattice (SAF-SkL) consisting of two antiferromagnetically coupled skyrmion lattice layers with opposite magnetic textures, modeled via a spin Hamiltonian including exchange, interlayer coupling, DMI, and anisotropy.
key_observables: Bulk and strip magnon spectra, layer polarization ($P_L$), edge mode localization ($\langle y angle / L_y$), and the spin Chern number ($C_s$).
important_parameters: Interlayer antiferromagnetic exchange stiffness ($A_{af}$), magnetization ($M_s$), Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction ($D$), and an external out-of-plane magnetic field ($h_z$).
main_assumptions: The analysis is based on the approximation of linear spin-wave theory and assumes a stabilized, relaxed bilayer texture from micromagnetic simulations.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the visual representation of the SAF-SkL; Fig 2 displays the strip magnon spectrum and edge mode localization; Fig 3 illustrates the evolution of the spectrum and transport properties as interlayer coupling varies.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the SAF-SkL system, presents the theoretical framework (BdG Hamiltonian and spin-wave theory), analyzes the bulk and edge spectra, performs symmetry analysis to classify the topological nature, and concludes with a unified framework for magnon Hall-type responses.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
